strategic_profile.executive_summary: Washington State University (WSU) is a prominent public land-grant research university in Pullman, Washington. Known for its 'very high research activity' (R1) designation and robust school spirit, WSU offers a classic, rural college town experience coupled with substantial hands-on undergraduate research opportunities.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Holistic but highly accessible; standardized test scores are explicitly not considered for admission, placing emphasis on unweighted GPA, curriculum strength (CADR requirements), and grade trends.

analyst_takeaways.insight: WSU operates on a 'certify into major' system for many competitive programs like Computer Science and Engineering, requiring students to maintain specific GPAs in prerequisite coursework during their freshman and sophomore years rather than being directly admitted.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants do not need to stress over hyper-competitive direct admissions for STEM, but must perform consistently well in college to declare their desired major.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The university boasts a highly spirited, tight-knit campus community heavily centered around Pac-12 (traditionally) athletics and Greek life, with 22% of students participating in fraternities and sororities.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Ideal for students seeking a classic, high-spirited residential college experience, though the rural location may feel isolating to those seeking immediate access to a major city.

analyst_takeaways.insight: WSU is exceptionally generous with scholarships, automatically considering applicants for merit awards (like the University Achievement Award or WUE) if they apply by the March 31 priority date.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Out-of-state students from western states (WUE) and strong in-state students can often attend at a highly competitive net price.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: A deeply rooted college town community defined by strong 'Cougar Pride', widespread Greek life involvement, and vibrant student organizations.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Situated in the rural Palouse region of eastern Washington, the campus is relatively isolated; while the campus is walkable, a car is highly recommended for traveling home or exploring beyond Pullman.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Classified as an R1 institution with over $350 million in annual research expenditures, undergraduates are highly encouraged to participate in fieldwork, lab research, and internships across disciplines.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-blind/Test-not-considered. WSU will neither require nor use SAT/ACT test scores for admission consideration or automatic scholarships. Submitted scores are only used for math/English course placement.

longitudinal_trends.notes: Waitlist data not publicly disclosed; WSU generally does not utilize a traditional waitlist for undergraduate admission.

gpa.notes: WSU admits students with a wide range of GPAs; a 3.44 is the reported average. Students with a 2.0-2.59 GPA are subject to additional holistic review.

testing.policy_note: Historical testing profile provided for context; WSU is completely test-blind for admissions purposes as of Fall 2022.

colleges.name: College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ideal for students seeking traditional liberal arts, humanities, or natural sciences degrees with access to extensive undergraduate research.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional residential halls required for first-year students.
colleges.student_archetype: Researchers, pre-meds, and liberal arts students.

majors.name: Psychology
majors.notes: The most popular major at WSU, awarding over 500 degrees annually.

colleges.name: Voiland College of Engineering and Architecture
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Designed for students who want a strong technical foundation and are capable of maintaining a solid GPA in prerequisite math and science courses to certify into their major.

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.special_requirements: Must certify into the major after passing introductory courses with a C or better and a minimum 2.5 cumulative GPA.
majors.notes: Students are not directly admitted as CS majors; they enter as pre-majors and apply to certify around their sophomore year.

majors.name: Mechanical Engineering
majors.special_requirements: Requires at least a C in all prerequisite courses to certify into the major.
majors.notes: A highly popular major at WSU, heavily integrated with hands-on labs and employer partnerships like Boeing.

colleges.name: College of Nursing
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For determined aspiring nurses who are prepared to handle intense science prerequisites before formally applying to the clinical BSN program.
colleges.housing_profile: Traditional housing, though clinical years may be located at WSU Spokane or other campuses.

majors.name: Nursing (BSN)
majors.special_requirements: Requires TEAS exam with a minimum score of 70, a minimum 3.0 prerequisite GPA, and individual prerequisite course grades of C (2.0) or higher. Interview required for final admission.
majors.notes: Students spend two years on prerequisites at Pullman, Tri-Cities, or via transfer, then apply for the final two years of clinical nursing coursework.

colleges.name: Carson College of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students seeking a comprehensive business education with strong networking opportunities in the Pacific Northwest.

majors.name: Marketing
majors.notes: One of the most popular degree paths at WSU.

colleges.name: Edward R. Murrow College of Communication
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: For students passionate about journalism, PR, and mass media. This is a flagship, globally recognized college.

majors.name: Communication
majors.special_requirements: Must maintain good academic standing and complete COM 300 with a C or better.
majors.notes: Students are admitted directly into their desired major in the College of Communication upon admission to WSU.

application_deadlines.notes: Priority consideration date for Fall semester. Applications accepted on a rolling basis afterward subject to space.
Priority date for automatic top academic scholarships.

supplemental_requirements.details: Not strictly required for most applicants, but strongly required/recommended for students with a 2.00 - 2.59 cumulative GPA to be considered through comprehensive holistic review.

holistic_factors.specific_differentiators: WSU is entirely test-blind. For students falling below a 2.6 GPA, the personal statement and grade trends become the primary vehicle for admission via comprehensive review.

major_selection_tactics: Apply generally: WSU evaluates applicants for admission to the university at large rather than restricting by specific major upon entry.

Understand 'Certifying': For popular STEM fields like CS and ME, you do not need to be a top-tier applicant out of high school; instead, you must maintain a 2.5+ GPA in your freshman/sophomore prerequisite classes to officially declare the major.

Nursing Pathway: Apply to WSU as a pre-nursing student, focus exclusively on maintaining a pristine GPA (above 3.0) and high TEAS score, and rely on the interview for final BSN admission in your sophomore year.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: WSU allows students to select alternate majors. Since most programs are pre-major entry, students who fail to certify into impacted programs (like Architecture or Engineering) are actively assisted by advisors to transition smoothly into alternate but related majors.

scholarships.name: University Achievement Award
scholarships.benefits: Tuition reduction for high-achieving in-state students.

scholarships.name: Cougar Award / WUE
scholarships.benefits: Significant tuition discount for out-of-state students from participating western states.

ap_policy.general_rule: Credit granted for scores of 3 and above.
exceptions: Language AP credits require taking a corresponding higher-level course at WSU for a grade of B or better to unlock the AP credits retroactively.
ap_policy.usage: Can be used to partially fulfill University Common Requirements (UCOREs) or lower-division prerequisites.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit awarded for scores of 4 or higher on both Standard Level (SL) and Higher Level (HL) exams.

tools: Direct Transfer Agreement (DTA) for Washington Community Colleges
transfer_articulation.restrictions: WSU does not accept credit by examination as transcripted by other institutions; official score reports must be sent directly from College Board or IB.

what_it_takes: Meeting the state CADR (College Academic Distribution Requirements)
A willingness to embrace a rural, college-town environment
essay_tips: Use the essay to explain any grade dips or anomalies if your GPA is below 2.6.
Showcase your readiness for a tight-knit community and your specific academic interests.
red_flags: Failing to complete Washington state's CADR high school requirements.
insights: WSU feels isolated to some (it's in the middle of eastern Washington farmland), but this breeds an incredibly strong internal community.
Dining options and mental health resources are areas students frequently feel need improvement.
Professors are generally highly approachable, heavily pushing undergraduate research opportunities.
